summary: In disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), prothrombin time (PT) and activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) may vary, especially in chronic cases where they can stay normal; however, d-dimers are consistently elevated. Clot solubility has no association with DIC and instead reflects Factor XIII deficiency, so it does not abnormally change in DIC—a critical distinction for identifying which lab result is an outlier.

Answer choice: D
